explanation: The Optimist presented more varied and dynamically evolving arguments, emphasizing new sectors and global reskilling successes. Their evidence (e.g., AI ethics roles, India’s AI logistics) showcased adaptability. The Pessimist’s logic was strong but overrelied on static displacement models. Both had high clarity, but the Optimist’s persuasive trajectory (from ‘myth’ to ‘ecosystems’) was more compelling.

factcheck_flags.claim: ILO’s 2025 report confirms a net loss of 10M positions
70% of displaced workers lack retraining access
factcheck_flags.issue: Attributed to World Bank 2024 without citation
